content.claim: Hungary has experienced repeated historical cycles of rural school expansion and contraction (interwar expansion, Stalinist demolition 1948+, 1970s rationalization) without establishing a durable institutionalized feedback mechanism, suggesting past sequences were driven by top-down political decisions rather than standing demographic-trigger rules.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Implementation planning lens
limitations: Sources do not specify whether any feedback loops existed within each historical cycle or only between cycles
Regime changes confound comparison of mechanism design across periods
No quantification of policy durability or reversibility for each cycle
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
